page: Amazon Web Services t4g.small is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 2 vCPUs based on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture (Neoverse-N1) running at 2.5 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the burstable nature and resource constraints of this instance. In single-core CPU testing via stress-ng, the processor achieves an average performance score of 1519.08, placing it in the middle 50% of tested hardware. However, multi-core CPU performance drops to the bottom 25% with a score of 3011.46. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large-block write speeds of 15.29 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 830.1, both ranking in the lower tiers. Cryptographic and compression tasks show similar constraints, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 20.34 GB/sec.

The t4g.small offers a highly cost-efficient profile for entry-level workloads, leveraging the power efficiency of ARM-based architecture to minimize operational costs. However, users must navigate significant tradeoffs, including the lack of local storage, a 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and a burstable CPU credit model that restricts sustained high-utilization workloads. This instance is best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, development and testing environments, and small-scale background tasks that require intermittent CPU bursts rather than continuous computational power.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.small is a burstable, budget-friendly instance powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 2.0 GB of system memory, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance is designed for low-intensity workloads. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.08, but multi-core and memory performance fall into the lower percentiles, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score of 830.1. The t4g.small represents a highly cost-efficient option for resource-light applications, but its burstable CPU allocation and limited memory capacity present clear tradeoffs for sustained workloads. It is best suited for microservices, small development environments, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.small is a burstable ARM64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. Powered by AWS Graviton, it offers cost-efficient performance for low-traffic web serving and microservices.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.small is a burstable ARM64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and AWS Graviton processors for budget-friendly workloads.
tagline: A budget friendly and burstable ARM64 virtual machine designed specifically for lightweight development workloads, testing environments, and low traffic microservices.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz
Features 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and Nitro hypervisor integration
Burstable CPU allocation suitable for intermittent processing needs
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Ideal for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and testing
